title: ricinocarpus plicatus
intro: Ricinocarpus plicatus, also known as the "paperbark maple" or "Japanese maple", is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Ricinocarpus plicatus is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating digestive issues, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
